rhythm: hero -> proof marquee -> what they're known for (stats inline) -> services -> who you deal with -> gallery -> reviews -> process -> coverage -> contact

sections.headline_intent: Trade-led: electrician + Bristol + the compliance/certified angle. Not a quote, not a backstory.
sections.image_reason: Labelled, certified consumer unit with a visible inspection/alarm record and dated board - finished, well-lit, no people, no clutter, and it embodies the compliance/EICR angle.

sections.items_source: 5.0 Google rating; 21 reviews; Bristol BS5; EICRs done within a week [src:reviews.Sandi]; Certificates handed over [src:reviews.Craig]; Pier & Jack on every job
sections.loop: duplicated track, translateX(-50%), linear, animates on all viewports

rows_intent: Certified and signed off - EICRs and the relevant certs handed over [src:reviews.Sandi][src:reviews.Craig]
Boards and circuits done properly - labelled consumer units, neat work [src:photos.01][src:photos.02][src:reviews.Dan]
Two electricians who turn up, finish on time, leave it tidy [src:reviews.Dan][src:reviews.Poppy]
sections.stats_note: All sourced (Google rating, review count, sourced opening hours = 24/7 in brief). No invented numbers, no photo counts.

sections.items_source: Real, evidenced electrical services - NOT the broad Google categories

sections.attribution: First name + 'Google review'; named people (Pier/Pierre, Jack) match brief.contact_name / review bodies

sections.map: keyless Google Maps iframe, q=Bristol BS5 (outward only, no street, no inward postcode)
sections.hours: Reachable seven days a week, including emergencies (brief: open 24 hours)
